Entity_Description: Tantallon is a suburban community in the Halifax Regional Municipality, Nova Scotia, with a population of roughly 6,000 at the 2021 Census. It sits at the head of St. Margaret's Bay, west of downtown Halifax along Highway 103 and the historic St. Margarets Bay Road, near the Route 333 junction toward Peggy's Cove. The area is dominated by newer residential subdivisions; neighbouring Upper Tantallon's Westwood Hills was the origin point of the May 2023 wildfire that destroyed 151 homes and forced roughly 16,400 evacuations. Most residents commute into Halifax Metro's technology, education, healthcare, military, and ocean-sciences economy.

Our crews know how salt air off St. Margaret's Bay wears on local properties. Call {PHONE}.
Short_Description: Few Nova Scotia communities have rebuilt as much, as fast, as Tantallon. After the May 2023 wildfire swept through neighbouring Upper Tantallon and the Westwood Hills subdivision — destroying 151 homes and forcing roughly 16,400 people to evacuate — this corner of St. Margaret's Bay has been in near-constant construction mode, with brand-new builds rising alongside established homes along St. Margarets Bay Road. That mix matters for {SERVICE}: a freshly rebuilt property and an older bay-side home have very different needs, and salt air off St. Margaret's Bay adds wear that inland properties never see. {COMPANY_NAME} serves all of Tantallon and the Highway 103 corridor with {SERVICE} matched to exactly these conditions. For your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimate from a local {BUSINESS_TYPE}, call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Tantallon sits where suburban Halifax meets the sea. Perched at the head of St. Margaret's Bay, west of downtown along Highway 103, this community of roughly 6,000 has grown from a quiet crossroads on the old St. Margarets Bay Road into one of the Halifax Regional Municipality's busiest suburban corridors — the point where traffic peels off toward Peggy's Cove on Route 333 and new subdivisions keep pushing back into the woods.

That growth is exactly why {SERVICE} in Tantallon is never a one-size-fits-all job. The housing stock spans established homes near the bay to entire streets of brand-new construction. Nowhere is that more visible than around the Westwood Hills subdivision in neighbouring Upper Tantallon, where the May 2023 wildfire — one of the most destructive in Nova Scotia's history — destroyed 151 homes and forced roughly 16,400 residents to evacuate. Three years on, the rebuild has given this area some of the newest housing in the province, and those properties deserve the same careful attention as the homes that stood here for decades.

The coastal setting adds demands of its own. Salt-laden wind rolls up St. Margaret's Bay year-round, quietly working on exterior surfaces, fasteners, and finishes. Maritime winters pile freeze-thaw cycles on top, while fog and wind-driven rain test every seam a property has. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} that treats Tantallon like an inland job will miss half of what actually causes problems here.

Most Tantallon residents commute into Halifax Metro's economy — technology, education, healthcare, the military, shipbuilding, and ocean sciences — which means busy households with little patience for contractors who don't show up. {COMPANY_NAME} was built for exactly these clients. {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} Our services include {SERVICE_LIST}, delivered with {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} and backed by {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of experience and a {RATING} rating.

Whether you're maintaining an established property near the bay, protecting a newly rebuilt home off Highway 103, or getting ahead of another Maritime winter, we respond within {RESPONSE_TIME} and start every project with a {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimate. {TAGLINE} — that's the standard we bring to every {PROPERTY_TYPE} in Tantallon. Call {PHONE} and {CTA_PRIMARY} today.

- The May 2023 Upper Tantallon wildfire destroyed 151 homes and forced roughly 16,400 evacuations, triggering a major rebuild around the Westwood Hills subdivision
- Housing stock mixes established bay-area homes with fast-growing newer subdivisions

- Coastal Maritime climate: salt air off St. Margaret's Bay combined with freeze-thaw winters
